description:
Enable codegen to consume the narrow specializations the typer recorded in `module_types.specs`. Each Term::Call / Term::TailCall site resolves to the SpecId matching its actual arg-Descr tuple; falls back to the any-key SpecId when no narrow spec exists. New compiled bodies appear per narrowed (FnId, input-key) pair.

This is where the per-callsite specialization optimization actually fires. CLIF diffs land here.

## Scope

- For every non-any-key spec in `module_types.specs`, register a new SpecId.
- Generate a Schema, Signature, FuncId, frame_size for each new SpecId.
- Compile each new SpecId's body via `compile_fn(spec_id, &module_types.spec(fn_id, &input_descrs))`.
- At each Term::Call / Term::TailCall, look up `(callee_fn_id, callsite_arg_descrs) → SpecId`. Emit the call against that SpecId's FuncId.
- Continuations: similar — `(cont_fn_id, [callee_return_descr, ...captured_descrs]) → SpecId`.

## Expected wins

Fns like `fact`, `add`, and other recursive int → int / float → float patterns get monomorphic compiled bodies; their cont frames disappear from typed flows (the cont specializations are also narrowed, slot 0 carries the callee's specialized return Descr — which is now narrow, not `any`).

## Acceptance

- Non-any-key SpecIds compiled and reachable from at least one Term::Call site.
- Fixture matrix green.
- CLIF diffs visible on typed-flow fixtures (fact, dist, add); spot-check that narrowed bodies emit fewer `fz_alloc_float` / cont-frame allocations.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.2.
title: .29.2.1: Per-callsite narrow-spec consumption
